title: Preparation process and use of snow crab hemocyanin
abstract: A process for preparing a sample that is substantially enriched of a marine-derived protein. The process comprises extracting and fractioning a physiological material obtained from a marine species. The marine-derived protein may be snow crab hemocyanin. It is used in pharmaceutical and bio-pharmaceutical applications.
claims:
Claims 1. Process for preparing a sample that is substantially enriched of a marine-derived protein, comprising extracting and fractioning a physiological material obtained from a marine species. 2. Process for preparing a sample that is substantially enriched of a marine-derived protein, comprising treating a physiological material obtained from a marine species, wherein the treatment comprises one or more of: sieving, filtration, freezing, addition of a preservative agent such as an antioxidant including but not limited to sulfites, thawing, dilution, centrifugation, pH adjustment, microfiltration and ultrafiltration. 3. Process for preparing a liquid sample that is substantially enriched of a marine-derived protein, comprising the steps of: (a) providing a physiological liquid material obtained from a marine species; (b) subjecting the material to at least one filtration (sieving) step to obtain a physiological liquid; (c) adjusting the pH of the liquid obtained at step (b) to an alkaline pH; (d) optionally, subjecting the liquid obtained at step (c) to a centrifugation step to yield a partially clarified aqueous fraction; (e) subjecting the aqueous fraction to a dilution step followed by a pH adjustment to an alkaline pH; and (f) subjecting the diluted aqueous fraction obtained at step (e) to at least one microfiltration step followed by at least one ultrafiltration step to yield the liquid marine-derived protein enriched sample. 4. Process according to claim 3, further comprising the following steps (a1) and (a2) performed sequentially right before or right after step (b): (a1) freezing the physiological liquid during a period of time; and (a2) thawing the frozen physiological liquid during a period of time. 5. Process for preparing a liquid sample that is substantially enriched of a marine-derived protein, comprising the steps of: 

 (a) providing a physiological liquid material obtained from a marine species; (b) subjecting the material to at least one filtration (sieving) step to obtain a physiological liquid; (a1) freezing the physiological liquid during a period of time; (a2) thawing the frozen physiological liquid; (c) adjusting the pH of the liquid obtained at step (a2) to an alkaline pH; (d) optionally, subjecting the liquid obtained at step (c) to a centrifugation step to yield a partially clarified aqueous fraction; (e) subjecting the aqueous fraction to a dilution step followed by a pH adjustment to an alkaline pH; and (f) subjecting the diluted aqueous fraction obtained at step (e) to at least one microfiltration step followed by at least one ultrafiltration step to yield the liquid marine-derived protein enriched sample. 6. Process for preparing a liquid sample that is substantially enriched of a marine-derived protein, comprising the steps of: (a) providing a physiological liquid material obtained from a marine species; (a1) freezing the material during a period of time; (a2) thawing the frozen material; (b) subjecting the material to at least one filtration (sieving) step to obtain a physiological liquid; (c) adjusting the pH of the liquid obtained at step (b) to an alkaline pH; (d) optionally, subjecting the liquid obtained at step (c) to a centrifugation step to yield a partially clarified aqueous fraction; (e) subjecting the aqueous fraction to a dilution step followed by a pH adjustment to an alkaline pH; and (f) subjecting the diluted aqueous fraction obtained at step (e) to at least one microfiltration step followed by at least one ultrafiltration step to yield the liquid marine-derived protein enriched sample. 7. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 6, further comprising a step of (g) freezing the liquid marine-derived protein enriched sample. 8. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, further comprising a step of (b1) adding a preservative agent to the physiological liquid obtained at step (b) prior to conducting step (c); preferably the preservative agent is an antioxidant such as sodium sulfite (Na 2 S0 3 ) or other forms of sulfites (sodium bisulfite, sodium metabisulfite), or ascorbic acid. 9. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein the physiological material at step (a) comprises a liquid and solids. 10. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein step (b) comprises using two or more sieves having mesh sizes of about 500 mesh (about 25 Î¼Î·Î¹). 11. Process according to any one of claims 4 to 6, wherein the temperature at step (a1) is about -30Â°C and the period of time is up to about 3 years. 12. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, further comprising the step of: (c1) storing the physiological liquid obtained at step (c) to a temperature of about 0Â°C to about 4Â°C, preferably at a temperature of about 2Â°C to about 3Â°C; for a period of time of about 12 hours to about 24 hours, preferably for a period of time of about 18 hours. 13. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein the alkaline pH at step (c) and the alkaline pH at step (e) are each independently about 8.0 to about 10.2, preferably about 8.8 to about 9.2. 14. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein step (c) comprises using a base compound, preferably the base compound is NaOH. 15. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein step (d) is conducted in a centrifuge, preferably in continuous and concentrating mode, preferably at a flow of about 40 L/h to about 80 L/h, more preferably at a flow of about 50 L/h to about 70 L/h; optionally, the centrifugal force is between about 5,000 x g to about 11 ,000 x g, preferably at about 10,000 x g. 16. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein step (d) is conducted at a temperature of about 25Â°C to about 37Â°C, preferably at a temperature of about 30Â°C to about 32Â°C; and step (d) is conducted for a period of time of up to about 30 minutes. 17. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein at step (d), substantially solid and/or lipid materials are removed from the physiological liquid. 18. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein the dilution process at step (e) comprises using an aqueous solution comprising a salt, preferably a 0.9% NaCI aqueous solution. 19. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein step (e) is conducted at a dilution factor of 2 or more times an initial volume of the physiological liquid material; preferably about 6 times to about 10 times, more preferably about 8 times. 20. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein step (f) comprises using microfiltration systems with cutoff membranes having sizes between about 4.0 Î¼Î·Î¹ to about 0.22 Î¼Î·Î¹, preferably about 0.3 Î¼Î·Î¹; optionally, a sequential microfiltration step is performed from larger to smaller cutoff membranes. 21. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7, wherein at step (f), the ultrafiltration step is conducted at about 100 kDa to about 10 kDa, preferably at about 50 kDa; optionally a single ultrafiltration step is conducted. 22. Marine-derived protein enriched sample obtained by the process as defined in any one of claims 1 to 21. 23. Marine-derived protein enriched sample in frozen form obtained by the process as defined in claim 7. 24. Process for preparing a substantially pure marine-derived protein, comprising purifying the liquid marine-derived enriched sample as defined in claim 22, wherein the purification process comprises one or more of: chromatography methods including but not limited to ion 

 exchange, size-exclusion, affinity chromatography; preferably, the purification process comprises anion exchange chromatography. 25. Process for preparing a substantially pure marine-derived protein, comprising defreezing the sample as defined in claim 23, wherein the purification process comprises one or more of: chromatography methods including but not limited to ion exchange, size- exclusion, affinity chromatography; preferably, the purification process comprises anion exchange chromatography. 26. Marine-derived protein obtained by the process as defined in claim 24 or 25. 27. Process according to claim 24 or 25, further comprising subjecting the protein to at least one diafiltration step including desalting and concentrating the protein in physiological buffer conditions, to obtain a non-sterile solution of the marine-derived protein. 28. Process according to claim 24 or 25, further comprising preparing a lyophilized form of the marine-derived protein. 29. Marine-derived protein as a non-sterile physiological solution obtained by the process as defined in claim 27. 30. Marine-derived protein in lyophilized form obtained by the process as defined in claim 28. 31. Process according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the marine species is a crustacean such as Chionoecetes opilio (snow crab), Megathura crenulata, Paralithodes camtschaticae, Cyanagraea praedator, Panaeus monodon, Limulus polyphemus, Concholepas concholepas, Haliotis iris, Homarus americanus, Pandalus borealis, Cancer magister, Callinectes sapidus, Carcinus aestuarii, Odontodactylus scyllarus, Paenus japonicus, Cancer irroratus. 32. Process according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the marine species is Chionoecetes opilio (snow crab) and the marine-derived protein is hemocyanin. 33. Process for preparing a sample that is substantially enriched of snow crab hemocyanin, comprising extracting and fractioning a physiological material obtained from snow crab. 34. Process for preparing a sample that is substantially enriched of snow crab hemocyanin, comprising treating a physiological material from snow crab, wherein the treatment comprises one or more of: sieving, filtration, freezing, addition of a preservative agent such as an antioxidant including but not limited to sulfites, thawing, dilution, centrifugation, pH adjustment, microfiltration and ultrafiltration. 35. Process for preparing a liquid sample that is substantially enriched of snow crab hemocyanin, comprising the steps of: (a) providing a physiological liquid material obtained from snow crab; (b) subjecting the material to at least one filtration (sieving) step to obtain a physiological liquid; (c) adjusting the pH of the liquid obtained at step (b) to an alkaline pH; (d) optionally, subjecting the liquid obtained at step (c) to a centrifugation step to yield a partially clarified aqueous fraction; (e) subjecting the aqueous fraction to a dilution step followed by a pH adjustment to an alkaline pH; and (f) subjecting the diluted aqueous fraction obtained at step (e) to at least one microfiltration step followed by at least one ultrafiltration step to yield the liquid snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample. 36. Process according to claim 35, further comprising the following steps (a1) and (a2) performed sequentially right before or right after step (b): (a1) freezing the physiological liquid during a period of time; and (a2) thawing the frozen physiological liquid during a period of time. 37. Process for preparing a liquid sample that is substantially enriched of snow crab hemocyanin, comprising the steps of: (a) providing a physiological liquid material obtained from snow crab; 

 (b) subjecting the material to at least one filtration (sieving) step to obtain a physiological liquid; (a1) freezing the physiological liquid during a period of time; (a2) thawing the frozen physiological liquid; (c) adjusting the pH of the liquid obtained at step (a2) to an alkaline pH; (d) optionally, subjecting the liquid obtained at step (c) to a centrifugation step to yield a partially clarified aqueous fraction; (e) subjecting the aqueous fraction to a dilution step followed by a pH adjustment to an alkaline pH; and (f) subjecting the diluted aqueous fraction obtained at step (e) to at least one microfiltration step followed by at least one ultrafiltration step to yield the liquid snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample. 38. Process for preparing a liquid sample that is substantially enriched of snow crab hemocyanin, comprising the steps of: (a) providing a physiological liquid material obtained from snow crab; (a1) freezing the material during a period of time; (a2) thawing the frozen material; (b) subjecting the material to at least one filtration (sieving) step to obtain a physiological liquid; (c) adjusting the pH of the liquid obtained at step (b) to an alkaline pH; (d) optionally, subjecting the liquid obtained at step (c) to a centrifugation step to yield a partially clarified aqueous fraction; (e) subjecting the aqueous fraction to a dilution step followed by a pH adjustment to an alkaline pH; and (f) subjecting the diluted aqueous fraction obtained at step (e) to at least one microfiltration step followed by at least one ultrafiltration step to yield the liquid snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample. 39. Process according to any one of claims 35 to 38, further comprising a step of (g) freezing the liquid snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample. 40. Process according to any one of claims 35 to 39, further comprising a step of (b1) adding a preservative agent to the physiological liquid obtained at step (b) prior to conducting step (c); preferably the preservative agent is an antioxidant such as sodium sulfite (Na 2 S0 3 ) and other forms of sulfites (sodium bisulfite, sodium metabisulfite), or ascorbic acid. 41. Snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample obtained by the process as defined in any one of claims 35 to 40. 42. Snow crab hemocyanin obtained by the process as defined in any one of claims 35 to 40. 43. Snow crab hemocyanin as a non-sterile physiological solution obtained by the process as defined in any one of claims 35 to 40. 44. Snow crab hemocyanin in lyophilized form obtained by the process as defined in any one of claims 35 to 40. 45. Snow crab hemocyanin according to any one of claims 41 to 44, having the amino acid composition profile outlined in Table 4. 46. Snow crab hemocyanin according to any one of claims 41 to 44, having an isoelectric point at a pH of about 4.65. 47. Snow crab hemocyanin according to any one of claims 41 to 44, having a molecular weight of about 74.9 kDa. 48. Snow crab hemocyanin according to any one of claims 41 to 44, having an amino acid sequence analogy with hemocyanin from other species, for example having about 21 % sequence homology with another crab hemocyanin (Cancer magister). 49. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier; optionally, the pharmaceutically acceptable carrier is a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier protein. 50. Method of stimulating the immune system in a mammal (human or animal), comprising using a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 or a pharmaceutical composition as defined in claim 49. 51. Method of treating various diseases including cancers in a mammal (human or animal), comprising administering to the mammal a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 or a pharmaceutical composition as defined in claim 49. 52. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 or a pharmaceutical composition as defined in claim 49 as immunostimulant or specific immunostimulant in a mammal (human or animal). 53. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 or a pharmaceutical composition as defined in claim 49 as immunogen or adjuvent in a mammal (human or animal). 54. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 or a pharmaceutical composition as defined in claim 49 for treating various diseases including cancers in a mammal (human or animal). 55. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 as carrier protein. 56. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 as alternative to a known carrier molecule such as KLH, BSA and ovalbumine. 57. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 in the preparation of a vaccine. 58. Use of a snow crab hemocyanin as defined in any one of claims 41 to 48 in the preparation of a medicament for treating various diseases including cancers. 59. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7 and 35 to 40, further comprising a step of (i) freezing the liquid marine-derived protein enriched sample or snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample during a period of time; (i') thawing the frozen sample to obtain a liquid; and (i") and freezing the liquid sample. 60. Process according to claim 59, wherein steps (i') and (i") are repeated a plurality of times. 61. Process according to any one of claims 3 to 7 and 35 to 40, further comprising a step of (i) freezing the liquid marine-derived protein enriched sample or snow crab hemocyanin enriched sample for later use, and wherein the sample survives a plurality of freeze-thaw cycles.
